tex:
(a) 40 football players vote on the colour of new shirts.
\newline

The results for red, yellow and green are shown in the bar chart.
\newline

(i) (b) Twice as many football players vote blue than vote orange.
\newline

Complete the bar chart.
\newline

(c) Write down the mode.
\newline

(d) 40 hockey players vote on the colour of new shirts.
\newline

The table shows the results.
\newline

(i) (e) Complete the pie chart.
\newline

(f) Work out the percentage of hockey players who vote grey.
\newline

qtype: timetable, speed-distance-time, fraction of a quantity, money calculations
tex:
(a) Here is part of the timetable for buses from the station to the city centre.
\newline

All buses take the same time to travel from the station to the city centre.
\newline

(i) (b) Complete the timetable.
\newline

(c) Beth walks 4 km from her home to the station at a speed of 6 km/h.
\newline

She wants to travel on the 09 24 bus.
\newline

Work out the latest time she can leave her home.
\newline

(d) 45 seats on the bus are occupied.
\newline
This is $\dfrac{3}{5}$ of the total number of seats on the bus.
\newline

Work out the total number of seats on the bus.
\newline

(e) Beth buys 2.4 kg of onions costing \$1.25 per kilogram and 4.5 kg of potatoes.
\newline
The total cost is \$11.64.
\newline

Find the cost of 1 kg of potatoes.
\newline

(f) (i) (g) One day 140 people enter a shop.
\newline
The ratio adults : children $= 3 : 2$.
\newline

Find the number of adults who enter the shop.
\newline

(h) The price of a television in this shop is \$624.
\newline
37.5\% of this price is profit.
\newline

Calculate the profit on this television.
\newline

(i) The price of a phone in this shop is \$420.
\newline
This price increases by 12\%.
\newline

Calculate the new price.
\newline

content.content:
Here is part of the timetable for buses from the station to the city centre.
\newline

All buses take the same time to travel from the station to the city centre.

Beth walks 4 km from her home to the station at a speed of 6 km/h.

\newline

45 seats on the bus are occupied.
\newline
This is $\dfrac{3}{5}$ of the total number of seats on the bus.
\newline

Work out the total number of seats on the bus.
\newline

Beth buys 2.4 kg of onions costing \$1.25 per kilogram and 4.5 kg of potatoes.
\newline
The total cost is \$11.64.
\newline

Find the cost of 1 kg of potatoes.
\newline

One day 140 people enter a shop.
\newline
The ratio adults : children $= 3 : 2$.
\newline

Find the number of adults who enter the shop.
\newline

The price of a television in this shop is \$624.
\newline
37.5\% of this price is profit.
\newline

Calculate the profit on this television.
\newline

This price increases by 12\%.

Calculate the new price.

tex:

\newline

On the grid, draw an enlargement of the trapezium with scale factor 3.
\newline

(b) The diagram shows four triangles, $A$, $B$, $C$ and $T$, and a point $P$ on a grid.
\newline

(i) (c) Write down the coordinates of point $P$.
\newline

( , )

(d) Describe fully the **single** transformation that maps

\begin{subsubparts}

(e) triangle $T$ onto triangle $A$

(f) triangle $T$ onto triangle $B$

(g) triangle $T$ onto triangle $C$.
\newline

\end{subsubparts}

Describe fully the \textbf{single} transformation that maps
triangle $T$ onto triangle $A$
triangle $T$ onto triangle $B$
triangle $T$ onto triangle $C$.

qtype: substitution, solving linear equations, expanding and simplifying algebraic expressions, rearranging formula, building expressions
tex:
(a) In a sport, teams are given points using the formula

number of points $=$ number of wins $\times 4 +$ number of draws $\times 2 +$ bonus points.
\newline
One team has 15 wins, 7 draws and 6 bonus points.
\newline

Calculate the total number of points for this team.
\newline

(b) Solve.
\newline

$\dfrac{x}{2} = 18$

$x = $ 

(c) Solve.
\newline

$4x + 12 = 18$

$x = $ 

(d) Expand and simplify.
\newline

$6(3x - 4) + 5(x - 2)$

(e) $T = 5r - 6$

Make $r$ the subject of this formula.
\newline

$r = $ 

(f) Bo has a green bag and a blue bag.
\newline
Each bag contains some marbles.
\newline

The green bag has $x$ marbles.
\newline
There are 5 times as many marbles in the blue bag than in the green bag.
\newline

Bo now adds 6 marbles to each bag.
\newline
There are now 4 times as many marbles in the blue bag than in the green bag.
\newline

Use this information to write down an equation and solve it to find the value of $x$.
\newline

$x = $

content.content:
In a sport, teams are given points using the formula

\begin{center}
number of points $=$ number of wins $\times 4 +$ number of draws $\times 2 +$ bonus points.
\newline
\end{center}

One team has 15 wins, 7 draws and 6 bonus points.
\newline

Calculate the total number of points for this team.
\newline

Solve.
\newline

$\dfrac{x}{2} = 18$

$x = $ \AnswerLine[][]

Solve.
\newline

$4x + 12 = 18$

$x = $ \AnswerLine[][]

Expand and simplify.
\newline

$6(3x - 4) + 5(x - 2)$

$T = 5r - 6$

Make $r$ the subject of this formula.
\newline

$r = $ \AnswerLine[][]

Bo has a green bag and a blue bag.
\newline
Each bag contains some marbles.
\newline

The green bag has $x$ marbles.
\newline
There are 5 times as many marbles in the blue bag than in the green bag.
\newline

Bo now adds 6 marbles to each bag.
\newline
There are now 4 times as many marbles in the blue bag than in the green bag.
\newline

Use this information to write down an equation and solve it to find the value of $x$.
\newline

$x = $ \AnswerLine[][]

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item In a sport, teams are given points using the formula

\begin{center}
number of points $=$ number of wins $\times 4 +$ number of draws $\times 2 +$ bonus points.
\end{center}

One team has 15 wins, 7 draws and 6 bonus points.

Calculate the total number of points for this team.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\item Solve.

$\dfrac{x}{2} = 18$

$x = $ \AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}

\item Solve.

$4x + 12 = 18$

$x = $ \AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\item Expand and simplify.

$6(3x - 4) + 5(x - 2)$

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\item $T = 5r - 6$

Make $r$ the subject of this formula.

$r = $ \AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\item Bo has a green bag and a blue bag.
Each bag contains some marbles.

The green bag has $x$ marbles.
There are 5 times as many marbles in the blue bag than in the green bag.

Bo now adds 6 marbles to each bag.
There are now 4 times as many marbles in the blue bag than in the green bag.

Use this information to write down an equation and solve it to find the value of $x$.

$x = $ \AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{5}

\end{parts}

tex:
(a) Li spins a fair 6-sided spinner numbered 1 to 6.
\newline

(i) (b) On the probability scale, draw an arrow ($\downarrow$) to show the probability that the spinner lands on the number 2.
\newline

(c) Find the probability that the spinner lands on a prime number.
\newline

(d) Find the probability that the spinner lands on the number 7.
\newline

(e) A bag contains 3 red balls and 12 green balls.
\newline
Li picks a ball at random.
\newline

Find the probability that it is a green ball.
\newline
Give your answer as a fraction in its simplest form.
\newline

(f) Li spins two fair 4-sided spinners, each numbered 1 to 4.
\newline
The two numbers are multiplied to give the score.
\newline

Find the probability that the score is

(i) (g) an even number

(h) an integer

(i) at least 10.
\newline

(j) A bag contains red discs and blue discs.
\newline
The probability that a disc picked at random is red is $\dfrac{1}{5}$.
\newline

Li picks a disc at random, notes its colour and then replaces it in the bag.
\newline
She then picks another disc at random.
\newline

(i) (k) Complete the tree diagram.
\newline

(l) Work out the probability that both of the discs she picks are blue.
\newline

content.content:
Li spins a fair 6-sided spinner numbered 1 to 6.
\newline

On the probability scale, draw an arrow ($\downarrow$) to show the probability that the spinner lands on the number 2.
\newline

Find the probability that the spinner lands on a prime number.
\newline

Find the probability that the spinner lands on the number 7.
\newline

A bag contains 3 red balls and 12 green balls.
\newline
Li picks a ball at random.
\newline

Find the probability that it is a green ball.
\newline
Give your answer as a fraction in its simplest form.
\newline

Li spins two fair 4-sided spinners, each numbered 1 to 4.
\newline
The two numbers are multiplied to give the score.
\newline
Find the probability that the score is
an even number
an integer

at least 10.
\newline

A bag contains red discs and blue discs.
\newline
The probability that a disc picked at random is red is $\dfrac{1}{5}$.
\newline

Li picks a disc at random, notes its colour and then replaces it in the bag.
\newline
She then picks another disc at random.
\newline

Complete the tree diagram.
\newline

Work out the probability that both of the discs she picks are blue.
\newline
